events.text: {"decision":"fail","risk":"high","work_stage":"tested/inspected","component_conditions":["installed-defective"],"percent_complete":80,"value":0,"sound_source":"","confidence":0.85,"event_time":0.0,"rate":0.0,"order":[],"workable":[],"findings":["Shaft alignment performed before final pipe connection, introducing pipe strain and invalidating alignment results (SEQ/TRD).","Final alignment not verified after piping tie-in; non-compliant with API 686 sequence requirements."],"actions":["Break final pipe connections, verify no pipe strain, and re-perform laser alignment after piping is fully connected and bolted.","Re-inspect and document final alignment values post pipe-up per API 686."],"rationale":"API 686 requires final shaft alignment to be performed after all piping is connected to ensure pipe strain does not distort machine casing or shaft alignment. Performing alignment before final pipe connection means the as-tested alignment is not representative of in-service conditions and is non-compliant.","references":["API 686","laser system manuals"]}
